prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. In 2022, tensions between the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Rwanda intensified, leading to multiple instances of Congolese and Rwandan forces attacking each other's territories. Rwandan forces have been reported to have crossed into the DRC multiple times, usually fighting alongside Congolese rebels. The crisis is linked to an ongoing offensive beginning in March 2022 by the March 23 Movement (M23), which the Congo and the United Nations accuses Rwanda of supporting and actively fighting. Rwanda and M23 also accuse the DRC of working alongside the FD
B. In 2022, heavy tensions broke out between the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Rwanda, which have led to several alleged attacks by Congolese and Rwandan forces on each other's territory. Rwandan forces have been caught crossing into the DRC multiple times, usually fighting alongside Congolese rebels. The crisis is related to an ongoing offensive beginning in March 2022 by the March 23 Movement (M23), which the Congo and the United Nations accuse Rwanda of not only supporting but actively fighting for. Rwanda and M23 have also accused the DRC of working together with the Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda (FDLR), a racist Hutu Power paramilitary group that took part in the Rwandan Genocide. Both the Congo and Rwanda deny they support the FDLR and M23,
C. During 2022, significant discord emerged between the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Rwanda, culminating in multiple purported assaults by Congolese and Rwandan military units on one another's land. Rwandan military personnel have been observed infiltrating the DRC on numerous occasions, frequently engaging in combat alongside Congolese insurgent groups. This predicament is linked to a continuing offensive that commenced in March 2022, orchestrated by the March 23 Movement (M23), an entity that both the Congo and the United Nations claim Rwanda is not only backing but also actively participating in the fight on its behalf. Concurrently, Rwanda and the M23 have leveled accusations against the DRC, alleging collaboration with the Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda (FDLR), a prejudiced Hutu Power paramilitary organization that played a role in the Rwandan Genocide. Both the Congo and Rwanda disavow any endorsement of the FDLR and M23, respectively.

Answer:

options.A: In 2022, tensions between the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Rwanda intensified, leading to multiple instances of Congolese and Rwandan forces attacking each other's territories. Rwandan forces have been reported to have crossed into the DRC multiple times, usually fighting alongside Congolese rebels. The crisis is linked to an ongoing offensive beginning in March 2022 by the March 23 Movement (M23), which the Congo and the United Nations accuses Rwanda of supporting and actively fighting. Rwanda and M23 also accuse the DRC of working alongside the FD
options.B: In 2022, heavy tensions broke out between the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Rwanda, which have led to several alleged attacks by Congolese and Rwandan forces on each other's territory. Rwandan forces have been caught crossing into the DRC multiple times, usually fighting alongside Congolese rebels. The crisis is related to an ongoing offensive beginning in March 2022 by the March 23 Movement (M23), which the Congo and the United Nations accuse Rwanda of not only supporting but actively fighting for. Rwanda and M23 have also accused the DRC of working together with the Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda (FDLR), a racist Hutu Power paramilitary group that took part in the Rwandan Genocide. Both the Congo and Rwanda deny they support the FDLR and M23,
options.C: During 2022, significant discord emerged between the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Rwanda, culminating in multiple purported assaults by Congolese and Rwandan military units on one another's land. Rwandan military personnel have been observed infiltrating the DRC on numerous occasions, frequently engaging in combat alongside Congolese insurgent groups. This predicament is linked to a continuing offensive that commenced in March 2022, orchestrated by the March 23 Movement (M23), an entity that both the Congo and the United Nations claim Rwanda is not only backing but also actively participating in the fight on its behalf. Concurrently, Rwanda and the M23 have leveled accusations against the DRC, alleging collaboration with the Democratic Forces for the Liberation of Rwanda (FDLR), a prejudiced Hutu Power paramilitary organization that played a role in the Rwandan Genocide. Both the Congo and Rwanda disavow any endorsement of the FDLR and M23, respectively.
